vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on cron.d
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/cron.d, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0700 /etc/cron.d
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then find -H /etc/cron.d/ -maxdepth 0 -perm /u+s,g+xwrs,o+xwrt -type d -exec chmod u-s,g-xwrs,o-xwrt {} \; else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

triage_decision.reason: Setting /etc/cron.d to 0700 is a standard STIG hardening step that does not affect authentication, networking, or critical services. It simply restricts access to the directory to the owner, which is acceptable for cron job management.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: filesystems affected; None identified; standard permission setting
